Title: Verify GET /users fails with 404 when endpoint is incorrect
Test Step: Send a GET request to the endpoint '/user' (misspelled version of '/users').
Step Expected: The API responds with a 404 status code and a meaningful error message indicating the endpoint was not found.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 422 when email is invalid
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "invalidemail@" }
Repeat with payload: { "name": "Jane Doe", "email": "test@.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 422 status code and an error message indicating that the email format is invalid.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 422 when name exceeds maximum character length
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "ThisNameIsWayTooLongAndExceedsTheMaximumAllowedCharacterLimitDefinedByApi", "email": "test@example.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 422 status code and an error message indicating that the 'name' exceeds the maximum allowed character limit.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 422 when email exceeds maximum character length
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "averylongemailaddresswhichexceedsthemaximumcharacterlimit@example.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 422 status code and an error message indicating that the 'email' exceeds the maximum allowed character limit.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 400 when 'name' has valid length but is an empty string
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "", "email": "test@example.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 400 status code and an error message indicating the 'name' field cannot be empty.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 400 when 'email' has valid length but is an empty string
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 400 status code and an error message indicating the 'email' field cannot be empty.

Title: Verify GET /users fails with 401 when authorization header is missing
Test Step: Send a GET request to the '/users' endpoint without an 'Authorization' header supplied.
Step Expected: The API responds with a 401 status code and an error message indicating that the request is unauthorized.

Title: Verify POST /users can handle special characters in 'name'
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "Ana María & López", "email": "ana@example.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 201 status code and the user is created successfully.

Title: Verify POST /users can handle valid edge-case email formats
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "user+tag@example.com" }
Step Expected: The API responds with a 201 status code and the user is created successfully.

Title: Verify GET /users handles a very large dataset
Test Step: Inject a very large dataset (e.g., 1 million users) into the users database.
Send a GET request to the '/users' endpoint.
If applicable, send a series of requests with pagination (e.g., '/users?page=1&limit=100').
Step Expected: The API responds with a 200 status code and returns the complete list of users as per pagination or all users if no pagination is applied.

Title: Verify POST /users does not allow duplicate emails
Test Step: Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the following payload:
{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "unique@example.com" }
Send another POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the same payload.
Step Expected: The API responds with a 400 status code and an error message indicating that the email already exists.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 413 when payload size exceeds the maximum limit
Test Step: Craft a very large payload (e.g., a 'name' or 'email' exceeding the maximum allowed payload size).
Send a POST request to the '/users' endpoint with the large payload.
Step Expected: The API responds with a 413 status code and an error message indicating the payload size exceeds the maximum limit.

Title: Verify GET /users supports query parameters for pagination
Test Step: Send a GET request to the '/users?page=1&limit=10' endpoint.
Step Expected: The API responds with a 200 status code and returns the first 10 users, along with pagination metadata if applicable.

Title: Verify GET /users with invalid query parameters fails gracefully
Test Step: Send a GET request to the endpoint '/users?page=-1&limit=10'.
Send a GET request to the endpoint '/users?page=1&limit=1000000'.
Step Expected: The API responds with a 400 status code or similar, along with an error message indicating invalid query parameter values.
